name: House Zootia

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Work-first courtesy and weather-crop conversation are common.
hospitality_rules: Guests are fed plainly but well when stores allow; livestock and field cycles shape generosity.
insult_triggers: treating raw production as lesser work
taboo_topics: boasting about urban luxury while depending on rural supply
class_expectations: producers are valued for reliability, herd judgment, and seasonal endurance
faith_etiquette: gratitude, renewal, and practical offering customs shape household devotion
outsider_mistakes: treating Zootia as background support rather than a decisive supply base

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: Daily life centers on farming, herding, shearing, supply movement, repair, and the seasonal management of output.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: The calendar matters directly through planting, lambing, shearing, and transport windows.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: Lanolin, cut grain, leather, stable heat, damp earth, and cooked stew define the ordinary field and market texture.

travel_and_transition_texture: Roads feel practical and supply-oriented, with wagon wear, resting animals, and seasonal track changes.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Homes are built around storage, animals, kitchens, work yards, and family labor interdependence.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for keeping animals, stores, and deliveries sound.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Zootia stories emerge as labor, provision, inheritance, weather pressure, and resentment toward those who consume without understanding production.
